stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is between 60% and 77% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is 87%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is 92%. What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.48 to 0.616 as the document verification pass rate varies from 60% to 77%. Point estimate: 0.552.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the document verification stage creates a 13.6 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the document verification rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
